title: Chef And Special Dishes

content.problemStatement:
One day, Chef prepared **D** brand new dishes. He named the i-th dish by a string **Si**. After the cooking, he decided to categorize each of these **D** dishes as special or not.



A dish **Si** is called special if its name (i.e. the string **Si**) can be represented in the form of a double string by removing at most one (possibly zero) character from it's name from any position. 



A string is called a double string if it can be represented as a concatenation of two identical, non-empty strings. 
e.g. "abab" is a double string as it can be represented as "ab" + "ab" where + operation denotes concatenation. 
Similarly, "aa", "abcabc" are double strings whereas "a", "abba", "abc" are not.
constraints: Sum of lengths of all Si does not exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: First line contains D, the number of dishes. Each of the next D lines contains a non‑empty string Si consisting of lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each dish, output a line containing "YES" if the dish name can become a double string after removing at most one character, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: aba -> delete the middle 'b' to obtain "aa" (double). abac is even but halves differ and any deletion makes length odd, so not special. abcd cannot become a double string with one deletion.
abcabc is already a double string. abc (length 3) after any single deletion becomes length 2 with two different characters, so not special.
abab is already a double string ("ab"+"ab"). No deletion needed.

solution.approach:
A string of even length is a double string iff its first half equals its second half. For an odd length string we may delete exactly one character. After deletion the length becomes even, so we only need to check whether we can align the two halves while skipping at most one character either from the left half or from the right half. This can be done with two‑pointer scans in O(|S|) time. The algorithm:
1. If |S| == 1 → NO.
2. If |S| is even → compare the two halves directly.
3. If |S| is odd → let mid = |S|/2. Try two scans:
   • Assume the deleted character lies in the left half: compare S[0..mid] with S[mid+1..] allowing one skip in the left pointer.
   • Assume the deleted character lies in the right half: compare S[0..mid-1] with S[mid..] allowing one skip in the right pointer.
   If either scan succeeds → YES, else NO.
The scan stops as soon as a second mismatch appears.
Time complexity per string is O(|S|); total O(sum of lengths). Only O(1) extra space is used.
